heading: Memorial addresses: illustrations
label: 724 Memorial addresses: illustrations
text: The illustrations to accompany bound copies of memorial addresses delivered in Congress shall be made at the Bureau of Engraving and Printing and paid for out of the appropriation for that bureau, or, in the discretion of the Joint Committee on Printing, shall be obtained elsewhere by the Director of the Government Publishing Office and charged to the allotment for printing and binding for Congress.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on 44 U.S. Code, 1964 ed., § 152 (, ).Mar. 4, 1921, ch. 161, § 141 Stat. 1431
Amendments2014— substituted “Director of the Government Publishing Office” for “Public Printer”.Pub. L. 113–235
